cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the Custom CSS section renders correctly
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > Custom CSS section.

steps.content: Open the Custom CSS section under Cookie Banner.
steps.expected: The section displays a Consent Template card at the top and an "Add your custom css here" card with a CSS text area below it.

steps.content: Note the plan-gating state of the CSS text area.
steps.expected:
- Basic plan and higher: text area is enabled and editable.
- Free plan: text area is locked with a premium icon (see 11. Billing & Upgrade > Plan Gates for the locked-state verification).

cases.rewrite_notes: Added retroactively per the new standing convention in migration-conventions.md §11 ("Plan-gated render cases"): a plan-gated feature that owns a real page/card (unlike a bare upgrade-icon stub) still gets a render-first case, and that case notes the plan-gating as a fact rather than re-verifying it (the full lock/nudge behavior stays in 37144, Plan Gates). Custom CSS was previously miscategorized in §11 as a "plan-gated stub" with no render surface — that exception has been narrowed. Not yet grilled live on QA2; spot-check the Consent Template + textarea structure description next /grill-section or /audit-section pass.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the Custom CSS editor is locked with an upgrade prompt on the Free plan
cases.preconditions:
User is on a Free plan.
User is on Cookie Banner > Custom CSS section. The section shows a Consent Template card at the top and an "Add your custom css here" card containing a CSS text area below it.

steps.content: Locate the "Add your custom css here" card in the Custom CSS section.
steps.expected: A premium/upgrade icon should be displayed next to the "Add your custom css here" label, indicating the feature is gated.

steps.content: Attempt to enter text in the custom CSS text area.
steps.expected: The text area should be disabled and should not accept any input on the Free plan.

steps.content: Click the premium/upgrade icon next to the "Add your custom css here" label.
steps.expected:
The Custom CSS upgrade prompt should be displayed with:
- "Style your banner with custom CSS"
- "Available in: Basic, Pro and Ultimate plans"
- A "Try Pro for free" call-to-action

cases.rewrite_notes: Collapsed 3 roles (Account Owner/Admin/Editor) across 7 Free-plan/platform sections. Stripped role/platform prefixes. Dropped nav steps, sidebar-list presence check, and Consent Template position checks (moved to preconditions), plus the tooltip-copy verification step. Kept premium-icon presence and disabled-textarea as the plan-gate signals. NOTE for /migrate-section: this is a plan-gate scenario (upgrade icon + locked state on a feature) and should be routed to 11. Billing & Upgrade > Plan Gates per convention section 6. Grilled on QA2 (Free account): confirmed the premium/crown icon appears next to the 'Add your custom css here' label and the CSS textarea is disabled ([disabled] in DOM). FIXED: the premium icon is interactive — clicking it opens an upgrade popover ('Style your banner with custom CSS', 'Available in: Basic, Pro and Ultimate plans', 'Try Pro for free' CTA); added a step to capture this nudge-opens behavior (feature section owns nudge-opens per section 6; the CTA destination belongs to section 11). The 'Try Pro for free' CTA was not clicked to avoid starting a trial. NOTE: the separate side upgrade-nudge panel on this page is a known bug per the user and was intentionally not captured.
cases.audit_note: Step 3's expected result had gone vague ("A description of the Custom CSS feature", "The plans it is available in: Basic, Pro and Ultimate") where this case's own rewrite_notes already documents the exact live-confirmed popover text ("Style your banner with custom CSS" / "Available in: Basic, Pro and Ultimate plans"). Restored the exact quotes from that grill confirmation. Note: Suite 6 source (C980/C1443, 2 of 19 sampled — identical) describes a hover TOOLTIP reading "Upgrade to Basic or a higher plan to unlock this feature.", not the v2 app's click-to-open popover; rewrite_notes says this tooltip-copy step was intentionally dropped since the v2 app's actual interaction (click, not hover) differs — that's a deliberate scope call already documented, not a fidelity bug, so left as-is.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the Custom CSS editor is available and editable on a paid plan
cases.preconditions:
User is on a paid plan (Basic or higher).
User is on Cookie Banner > Custom CSS section. The section shows a Consent Template card at the top and an "Add your custom css here" card below it.

steps.content: Locate the "Add your custom css here" card in the Custom CSS section.
steps.expected: The card should be displayed with a custom CSS text area and no premium/upgrade icon on the label.

steps.content: Click into the custom CSS text area.
steps.expected: The text area should be enabled and accept keyboard input.

cases.rewrite_notes: Rewrote the Trial-with-card "Display of Custom CSS section" cases (3 roles) as an element-presence/access check per convention section 4. Stripped role/platform prefixes, dropped nav/sidebar/position steps. The absence of the premium icon and the enabled textarea are the behavioral contrast to the Free-plan locked case. Grilled on QA2 (Basic trial account): Custom CSS card renders with an editable textarea and no premium icon on a paid plan; textarea accepts input.
cases.audit_note: Sampled 2 of 3 source_case_ids (C4844, C4846 — identical). "Add your custom css here" card/label quote matches source verbatim; no premium icon and enabled textarea correctly reflect the paid-plan contrast case.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that custom CSS entered in the editor is applied to the banner and saved on publish
cases.preconditions:
User is on a paid plan (Basic or higher).
User is on Cookie Banner > Custom CSS section with the "Add your custom css here" text area enabled.

steps.content: Enter valid custom CSS into the "Add your custom css here" text area.
steps.expected: The banner preview should update to reflect the entered custom CSS (the styled property is applied to the banner in the preview canvas). The "Publish Changes" button should become enabled.
The changes should be saved (the "Publish Changes" button becomes disabled) and the custom CSS should be applied to the banner on the live website.

cases.rewrite_notes: Collapsed the full Stripe upgrade sequence and the Admin/Editor team-invite setup into the paid-plan precondition per convention section 2c. Collapsed 3 roles across plan/platform variants. Stripped prefixes, dropped nav/sidebar/position steps. Inferred publish outcome: the source expected result read "US State Laws banner should not be displayed on the user website", a copy-paste error unrelated to Custom CSS; rewrote to the correct behavioral outcome. Grilled on QA2 (Basic trial): verified custom CSS injects into the preview iframe live (.cky-consent-container computed background-color changed to the entered value) and Publish saves (button disables; a post-publish 'Publishing Cookie Banner' dialog appears prompting script install if the site isn't installed). FIXED: corrected 'banner preview in the sidebar' to the main preview canvas (the preview is the right-hand canvas, not the sidebar), and made the publish outcome match observed behavior.
cases.audit_note: Sampled 2 of 22 source_case_ids (C981, C1444 — identical). "Add your custom css here" and "Publish Changes" quotes match source verbatim. The source's copy-paste error ("US State Laws banner should not be displayed") was already correctly identified and rewritten in rewrite_notes — not a fidelity loss.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that saved custom CSS persists after reload
cases.preconditions:
User is on a paid plan (Basic or higher).
User is on Cookie Banner > Custom CSS section with the "Add your custom css here" text area enabled.

steps.content: Enter valid custom CSS into the "Add your custom css here" text area and click "Publish Changes".
steps.expected: The changes should be saved and the "Publish Changes" button should become disabled.

steps.content: Reload the Cookie Banner page (or log out and log back in) and open the Custom CSS section.
steps.expected: The previously saved custom CSS should still be present in the "Add your custom css here" text area.

cases.rewrite_notes: Gap found during grill (QA2, Basic trial). No draft case covered persistence of saved custom CSS. Verified live: after entering '.cky-consent-container { background-color: rgb(255, 0, 0); }', publishing, then logging out and back in, the textarea still held the published value and remained enabled. Complements the apply-and-publish case.
cases.audit_note: Gap case, no source_case_ids. Does quote "Add your custom css here" and "Publish Changes" labels, but these match the same labels already verbatim-confirmed against Suite 6 source in 37141/37142 in this same file. Persistence-after-reload behavior itself was confirmed live during grilling per rewrite_notes (grill_status: suggested, not yet promoted to confirmed) — worth a live spot-check if this case hasn't been re-verified since.
